title: American cinnamon rolls
subtitle: Soft enriched rolls with cinnamon filling and cream cheese frosting
excerpt: American cinnamon rolls use a very enriched dough (eggs, butter, milk, and sugar) so they bake up pillowy rather than bready. The cream cheese frosting goes on while the rolls are still warm so it melts partly into the swirls.
sourceNotes: The American cinnamon roll is documented in US domestic cookery from the early twentieth century, popularised through church fundraiser cookbooks and regional baking traditions in the Midwest. The enriched rolled bun with cinnamon-sugar filling and cream cheese frosting is the definitive modern form, widely published by the 1970s.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled, without frosting. Defrost at room temperature and warm in a low oven before frosting.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble and refrigerate overnight after shaping. Bring to room temperature for 1 hour before baking.

glossaryTerms.definition: A frosting made from cream cheese, butter, and icing sugar. Softer and less sweet than pure buttercream, with a slight tang that balances the sweetness of cinnamon-sugar filling. Applied to warm baked goods so it melts into the surface.

content.text: The dough for American cinnamon rolls is much richer than a standard bread dough — the eggs and butter give it a soft, pull-apart texture that holds its moisture for a day or two after baking. The
goes on while the rolls are warm from the oven, which melts it slightly into the swirls. Let the rolls cool for 10 minutes first so the frosting doesn't run completely off.
Mix, knead, and bulk ferment, 1 hour

Combine {{strong-bread-flour}}, {{yeast-fast-action}}, {{salt-table}}, and {{caster-sugar}}. Add the {{whole-milk}}, {{eggs}}, and {{unsalted-butter}} from the dough group. Mix to a soft, slightly sticky dough. Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic — enriched doughs take longer to come together than plain ones. Cover and leave until doubled, about 1 hour.

Mix the filling {{light-brown-sugar}} and {{cinnamon-ground}} together. Roll the dough into a rectangle roughly 30 × 45 cm. Spread the filling {{unsalted-butter}} over the surface, then scatter the cinnamon sugar mix, pressing lightly so it sticks. Roll tightly from the long edge into an even cylinder. Cut into 9 equal rounds. Line a rectangular tin (roughly 23 × 30 cm) with baking paper and arrange the rolls cut-side up with a little space between them. Cover and prove for 30 minutes until touching and visibly risen. Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan).

Bake for 22–25 minutes until pale gold on top — these rolls colour less than a standard bread. While they cool, beat {{cream-cheese}} with the frosting {{unsalted-butter}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} until smooth. Spread thickly over the rolls after 10 minutes out of the oven.
